description:
Fourth subticket. Depends on .16.3.

The user-facing feature: an ExUnit-shaped test framework atop the substrate from .16.1-.16.3.

## Scope

- New module fixtures/test_lib.fz (or a small built-in equivalent) defining:
  - defmodule Test do; defmacro test(name, do_body), do: <quote producing fn _test_<gensym>(), do: do_body + registration> end
  - assert(cond) / assert_eq(a, b) / assert_neq(a, b) builtins (probably interp-level).
- Auto-discovery: any fn whose name starts with `_test_` (or matches the macro's mangled output) is a test.
- `fz test <path>` subcommand: load + expand + flatten, find tests, run each in isolation, report pass/fail with assertion messages. ExUnit-style summary line.

## Out of scope

- Tags / filters / setup blocks / on_exit / capture_log — file follow-ups if useful.
- Test contexts (`test "name", %{key: val}, do: ...`) — depends on map-arg patterns at call sites.

## Tests

- A handful of sample test fixtures (passing + failing) under fixtures/tests/.
- An integration test that invokes `fz test` and asserts the report.
title: test/assert + fz test runner
